notes: Model uses deterministic synthetic latency and availability draws; values are comparative, not SLA guarantees.
Current baseline reflects existing mirrored relay fanout with quorum merge and wait-for-all completion.
PIR candidate models a two-server non-colluding design with heavy CPU/bandwidth overhead.

strategies.summary: Current production path: direct client fanout to mirrored relays with quorum merge

privacy.interpretation: Direct endpoint-to-relay fetch; strongest simplicity, weakest endpoint metadata shielding.

operational_notes: No extra trust domain; relies on independent relay operators and quorum policy.
Client endpoint metadata is still directly exposed to every queried relay.

strategies.summary: Client queries a privacy proxy, proxy fans out to relay mirrors and returns merged result

privacy.interpretation: Moderate metadata improvement by hiding endpoint from relays, with central proxy trust trade-off.

operational_notes: Adds one trusted privacy proxy blast-radius; proxy compromise can re-link clients to receivers.
Improves relay-side unlinkability but increases infrastructure complexity and abuse-governance load.

strategies.summary: Research candidate: two-server PIR-style retrieval mediated by proxy

privacy.interpretation: Best theoretical receiver-interest protection, but only if non-colluding servers and high compute budget hold.

operational_notes: Strongest receiver-interest privacy under non-collusion assumption.
Current performance/cost envelope is not suitable for always-on mobile polling path.

recommendation.decision: NO-GO for mandatory PIR/proxy production rollout now; GO for optional research profile behind explicit operator flag
rationale: Proxy fanout improves endpoint exposure score from 1.000 to 0.520, but introduces a concentration trust domain.
Two-server PIR candidate reduces receiver-interest exposure to 0.270, but adds 6.7x infra CPU and 5.0x client downlink versus baseline.
PIR availability (0.930) materially underperforms baseline (1.000) under realistic relay/proxy outage assumptions.
next_steps: Keep current multi-relay split retrieval as default with stronger relay diversity and anti-correlation controls.
Prototype proxy fanout only in high-risk mode with strict no-log attestations, jurisdiction split, and external audit before rollout.
Revisit PIR if practical latency can stay within +35% p95 and infra CPU within 3x baseline under mobile polling constraints.
